response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but his profile is a poor match for this C++ Senior Manager role in industrial automation. His C++ exposure is minimal and dated—limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor thesis project. The core requirements—deep C++/STL expertise, multi-threaded systems design, industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), Secure Development Lifecycle per IEC 62443-4-1, and industrial automation domain knowledge—are largely absent. His strengths lie in web development, event-driven web platforms, and developer tooling, none of which translate directly to the embedded/industrial engineering context this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of software experience with demonstrated adaptability across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Event-driven architecture experience with GISCollective platform and high-throughput micro-services work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and agile methodologies
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with no evidence of modern C++/STL or multi-threaded systems proficiency
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded systems experience relevant to the role; no exposure to industrial protocols such as MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, or IEC 61850
No mention of Secure Development Lifecycle practices, IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, or UML/architecture documentation required for this position

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IoT domain knowledge
Strong agile and software craftsmanship practices
